input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS targeting iOS and Android.

---

## 1. Install Xcode (iOS requirement)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- Install the **latest stable Xcode** (currently Xcode 16.x).
- After install, run:
  ```
  xcode-select --install          # installs CLI tools
  sudo xcodebuild -license accept # accepts license
  ```
- **Verify:** `xcodebuild -version`

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest **.NET 8 SDK** (or .NET 9 if you want the newest MAUI features).
- Use the **macOS installer** (`.pkg` for Arm64 or x64 depending on your Mac).
- **Discover versions:** The download page shows the current/LTS SDK versions. MAUI ships with .NET, so the SDK version determines your MAUI version.
- **Verify:** `dotnet --version` and `dotnet --list-sdks`

## 3. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

- This pulls in `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, and all dependencies.
- **Discover available workloads:** `dotnet workload search maui`
- **Verify:** `dotnet workload list` — should show `maui` as installed.

## 4. Install JDK (Android requirement)

- The MAUI Android workload bundles a JDK automatically, but if you need one explicitly:
  - **Source:** [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download) (recommended) — JDK 17.
  - Alternatively: `brew install microsoft-openjdk@17`
- **Verify:** `java -version`

## 5. Android SDK & Emulator

The MAUI workload auto-provisions Android SDK components on first build, but you can manage them explicitly:

- **Option A (recommended):** Let `dotnet build` auto-download everything.
- **Option B:** Install **Android Studio** from [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) for the SDK Manager, Emulator, and device manager GUI.
- Key components needed: Android SDK Platform 34+, Build-Tools, Platform-Tools, Android Emulator, and a system image.
- **Verify:** `$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/sdkmanager --list`

## 6. (Optional) IDE

| IDE | Source |
|-----|--------|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit + .NET MAUI extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

> *Visual Studio for Mac has been retired.* VS Code with the MAUI extension or Rider are the current macOS options.

## 7. Verify the Full Toolchain

```bash
dotnet new maui -n TestApp
cd TestApp

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on iOS Simulator
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios -t:Run -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=<SIMULATOR_UDID>

# Run on Android Emulator
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Run
```

If all four commands succeed, your environment is fully working.

## Quick Checklist

1. ✅ Xcode + CLI tools + license accepted
2. ✅ .NET SDK (8 or 9) via official installer
3. ✅ `dotnet workload install maui`
4. ✅ JDK 17 (auto-provisioned or manual)
5. ✅ Android SDK (auto-provisioned or via Android Studio)
6. ✅ IDE of choice (VS Code + extensions or Rider)
7. ✅ Scaffold & build a test app for both platforms
